---
format: html
title: get_trait_domain_names_from_vegvault()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>get_trait_domain_names_from_vegvault</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Get Trait Domain Names from VegVault</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Queries the VegVault SQLite database to retrieve all unique trait
domain names from the 'TraitsDomain' table.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>get_trait_domain_names_from_vegvault(
  path_to_vegvault = here::here("Data/Input/VegVault.sqlite"),
  verbose = TRUE
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="path_to_vegvault">path_to_vegvault</code></td>
<td>
<p>A character string specifying the path to the VegVault SQLite
database (default: 'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/VegVault.sqlite&quot;)').</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="verbose">verbose</code></td>
<td>
<p>Logical. If 'TRUE' (default), progress messages are printed to
the console via 'cli'. Set to 'FALSE' to suppress all console
output.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The function performs the following steps:
</p>
<p>1. Validates input parameters.
2. Checks the presence of the VegVault SQLite database using
'check_presence_of_vegvault()'.
3. Opens a DBI connection to the SQLite database.
4. Queries the 'TraitsDomain' table for distinct
'trait_domain_name' values and collects results.
5. Closes the database connection.
6. Removes 'NA' values from the result.
7. Asserts that at least one domain name was found.
8. Optionally logs the result via 'cli::cli_inform()'.
9. Returns the character vector of domain names.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>A character vector of unique, non-'NA' trait domain names found
in the 'TraitsDomain' table.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[extract_traits_from_vegvault()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
